sourceTitle: Customize your Notion Agent with instructions & skills

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of 'Instructions' for your Notion Agent?

options.text: To save a prompt you can run manually later
options.explanation: That describes a 'Skill'. Instructions define the default behavior of the Agent.

options.text: To set default rules for tone, formatting, and style
options.explanation: Correct. Instructions tell your Agent how to respond by default in every chat.

options.text: To organize and share database views
options.explanation: Database views are managed in the database settings, not via Notion Agent instructions.

options.text: To automatically run tasks without user input
options.explanation: Skills can be run manually, though the Agent may suggest them; Instructions control the Agent's personality, not automatic execution.

questions.question: How do you create a new Skill from an existing page?

options.text: Click the share button and select 'Set as Skill'
options.explanation: Sharing a page makes it accessible to others, but does not automatically convert it into a reusable Skill.

options.text: Click ... → Use with AI → Set as skill
options.explanation: Correct. This menu option converts a specific page into a Skill that you can reuse.

options.text: Rename the page to include the word 'Skill' in the title
options.explanation: Simply renaming the title does not register it as a Skill in the system.

options.text: Drag and drop the page into the Notion AI sidebar
options.explanation: Skills are pages; you don't drag them to a sidebar, but rather run them from the chat or text selection menu.

questions.question: Where can you view your current instructions and manage your skills?

options.text: Settings → Notion AI → General
options.explanation: Correct. This is the central hub to switch instructions, manage skills, and discover shared skills.

options.text: The Notion Agent settings within a specific chat
options.explanation: While you can switch instructions in chat, the central management console is in Settings.

options.text: The specific page where the instructions are written
options.explanation: You can remove an instruction page from its own settings, but you cannot view or manage all instructions/skills there.

options.text: Workspace → Members
options.explanation: Permissions and workspace settings are different from Notion AI specific configurations.

questions.question: Which statement is true regarding the sharing of Skills?

options.text: Shared skills automatically update everyone's Notion Agent behavior
options.explanation: Sharing a skill page allows others to run it, but it does not change their Agent's default instructions.

options.text: If someone has access to the skill page, they can run the skill
options.explanation: Correct. Access to the page grants the ability to run the skill. If they have edit permissions, their edits will change the skill for everyone.

options.text: You cannot share skills with people outside your workspace
options.explanation: Sharing works like any other Notion page, so you can share with people inside or outside your workspace.

options.text: Shared skills appear as built-in options in the text menu automatically
options.explanation: While you can designate custom skills to appear in the text menu in General settings, simply sharing a page does not auto-populate menus.

questions.question: How many 'Instructions' pages can be active at the same time?

options.text: Unlimited
options.explanation: You cannot have multiple active instruction pages simultaneously.

options.text: One
options.explanation: Correct. You can only use one instructions page at a time, though you can switch between them anytime.

options.text: One per user role (Admin vs. Member)
options.explanation: The limitation is per user account, not per role.

options.text: None, you must use the default system instructions
options.explanation: You can create custom instructions pages to override default behavior.

questions.question: How do you run a Skill in the chat interface?

options.text: Select the skill name from the sidebar
options.explanation: Skills are not in the sidebar; they are pages or chat commands.

options.text: Type @ followed by the name of the skill page
options.explanation: Correct. The @ symbol triggers the ability to select a specific skill page.

options.text: Double-click the skill page in the workspace
options.explanation: Double-clicking a page opens it; you must run it via AI to use its content.

options.text: Drag the skill page into the chat input box
options.explanation: Skills are not draggable elements; they are invoked via commands or menus.

questions.question: Can you use Notion AI Instructions for a 'Custom Agent'?

options.text: Yes, the instructions apply to all agents in the workspace
options.explanation: Notion Agent instructions only apply to your personal Notion Agent, not Custom Agents.

options.text: No, Custom Agents have their own separate instructions and permissions
options.explanation: Correct. Custom Agents are configured separately with their own specific setup.

options.text: Only if you share your instructions page with the Custom Agent
options.explanation: Sharing a page does not grant an AI agent access to your personal instruction settings.

options.text: Yes, but only for text editing tasks
options.explanation: The instruction settings are exclusive to the Notion Agent and do not apply to Custom Agents.

questions.question: What is the best practice for writing effective instructions?

options.text: Start with what you don't want
options.explanation: The article suggests starting with what you want, not what you don't want.

options.text: Be specific, especially regarding formatting
options.explanation: Correct. Being specific helps the Agent understand exactly how to format or behave.

options.text: Keep the instructions very long to cover all edge cases
options.explanation: You should keep instructions short; you can always add more detail later.

options.text: Use complex technical jargon for precision
options.explanation: Clear, plain language is usually better for guiding the AI's behavior.

questions.question: How can you designate which skills appear in the text selection menu?

options.text: By typing @ in the text selection menu
options.explanation: @ is used to run specific skills in chat, not to configure the menu.

options.text: By going to Settings → Notion AI → General
options.explanation: Correct. You can designate custom skills to show up in the text selection menu in the General settings.

options.text: By pinning them to the sidebar
options.explanation: Skills are not pinned to the sidebar; they appear in menus when invoked.

options.text: By writing the skill prompt inside the text itself
options.explanation: The text selection menu displays pre-defined skills, not the text content of the prompt.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a 'Skill'?

options.text: ‘Summarize this meeting for me in bullet points’
options.explanation: This is an example of an Instruction (a rule to follow) rather than a reusable prompt saved as a skill.

options.text: ‘Turn meeting notes into a follow-up email’
options.explanation: Correct. Skills are reusable prompts for tasks like 'Reformat' or 'Rewrite'.

options.text: ‘Keep my tone professional’
options.explanation: This is an example of an Instruction (setting a tone preference).

options.text: ‘Always end with a checklist’
options.explanation: This is an example of an Instruction (a rule to follow).
